title: platform-user — published events
description: Every platform-user lifecycle event fires after DB commit so downstream listeners never see uncommitted state. Payloads are readonly value objects (readonly class + readonly properties) — immutable by construction so listeners cannot mutate the shape another listener depends on. Payloads carry `platform_user_id` only; downstream services that need PII (email, phone, department) re-fetch through PlatformUserRepository so redaction rules apply consistently. The `notifications-critical` queue is used for offboarding-related events which MUST NOT queue behind a backlog (the 24h SLA depends on prompt processing).

events.name: PlatformUserHired
events.trigger: New PlatformUser row inserted after passing the HirePlatformUserInput safety gates (MFA enrolled on Identity, unique identity_id). Fires exactly once per PlatformUser, ever.

events.name: PlatformUserActivated
events.trigger: PlatformUser.status transitioned from `pending` to `active` (usually via the onboarding checklist completing) OR from `suspended` back to `active` via `restore`.

events.name: PlatformUserSuspended
events.trigger: PlatformUser.status transitioned from `active` to `suspended`. Temporary state — for investigations, leaves of absence, or reversible policy violations. Distinct from `offboarded` (terminal).

payload.reason: string (max 1000 chars; captured at suspend time)

events.name: PlatformUserRestored
events.trigger: PlatformUser.status transitioned from `suspended` back to `active`. Idempotent — restoring an already-active row is a no-op that emits no event.

payload.previous_reason: string (max 1000; the reason that suspended them)

events.name: PlatformUserOffboarded
events.trigger: PlatformUser.status transitioned to `offboarded` (terminal). offboarded_at populated. Dispatches RevokePlatformUserAccessJob synchronously in the same request-cycle so the 24h SLA is measured from event emission.

payload.effective_at: ISO-8601 timestamp (may be future-dated up to 30 days)

events.name: PlatformProfileUpdated
events.trigger: One or more columns on the PlatformProfile row mutated. Fires once per save regardless of the number of changed columns. Includes the changed field names so listeners can filter (e.g. `avatar_updated` listener only reacts when `avatar_url` is in the changed set).

payload.changed_fields: string[] (subset of: first_name, last_name, display_name, avatar_url, phone_e164, slack_handle, github_handle, timezone)
payload.actor_id: ULID (the PlatformUser themselves for self-service, OR the super_admin for admin-driven writes)

events.name: PlatformUserManagerReassigned
events.trigger: PlatformUser.manager_id mutated from a non-null value to a different non-null value, or to null (orphaning to top-of-tree), or from null to a non-null value (adopting into a chain).

payload.actor_id: ULID (usually a super_admin during a re-org)

events.name: PlatformUserDepartmentChanged
events.trigger: PlatformUser.department mutated. Emitted separately from the generic profile update so downstream services with per-department routing (Slack channel selection, on-call rotation membership) can react cleanly.
